task_completion.description: The space is divided by partition walls into at least 4 distinct functional zones (Lobby, Auditorium, Backstage, Restrooms) with defined room areas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D floor plan shows walls creating separate enclosed rooms. The rooms should be logically identifiable or labeled as a Lobby/Box Office, Auditorium, Backstage (with dressing rooms), and Restrooms.

task_completion.description: The Stage area within the Auditorium has a distinct floor color or texture to visually separate it from the seating area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, a specific rectangular area (the stage) inside the Auditorium has a different floor color or pattern compared to the rest of the Auditorium floor.

task_completion.description: At least 4 doors are installed to ensure logical traffic flow between the zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D floor plan shows at least 4 door icons (with swing arcs) connecting the Lobby, Auditorium, Restrooms, and Backstage areas.

task_completion.description: The Auditorium is furnished with at least 30 chairs arranged in rows facing the stage.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D plan or 3D view shows a large block of chairs (approximately 30 or more) arranged in clear rows, all oriented towards the distinct stage area.

task_completion.description: The layout includes at least 3 desks/tables, 2 storage items, and 2 plumbing fixtures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D plan or 3D view shows desks/tables (e.g., in the lobby or backstage), storage items like wardrobes/cabinets (e.g., backstage), and plumbing fixtures like toilets/sinks (in the restrooms).

task_completion.description: A 3D photo rendering of the auditorium showing the seating and stage is generated and saved to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'auditorium_render.png' is visible on the Desktop, and an image viewer shows it depicts a 3D view of the auditorium with the chairs and stage.

task_completion.description: The completed Sweet Home 3D project file is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sweet Home 3D window title bar shows the filename without an asterisk (indicating no unsaved changes), or the file manager shows the updated .sh3d file.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D GUI to design the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sweet Home 3D application open with the floor plan actively being edited, rather than just terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: Furniture and walls were placed manually using the application's tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the agent dragging and dropping items from the catalog and drawing walls, rather than loading a pre-completed file.

integrity.description: The 3D render was generated using Sweet Home 3D's built-in rendering tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Create photo' dialog in Sweet Home 3D is visible at some point, and the final render perfectly matches the 3D view of the designed floor plan.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected render must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Desktop/auditorium_render.png'. The starter file is 'theater_starter.sh3d', which is initially completely empty of furniture, rooms, and labels. Any presence of these items indicates the agent successfully added them.
